details.intent: Fetch the full page for https://example.com and extract (A) the exact H1 text, (B) the exact external link URL(s) present on the page and their domain(s), and (C) supply the page's URL as a citation.
target_facts: exact_h1_text: string (the content of the first H1 element, exact punctuation/case)
external_link_url: string (the exact href of the external link found on the page; if multiple, return first listed)
external_link_domain: string (the domain portion of external_link_url)
page_citation: string (the exact visited URL to cite: https://example.com)
details.extraction_plan: 1) Load the page at https://example.com. 2) Parse and extract the exact text of the first H1 element (preserve whitespace/punctuation). 3) Find anchor elements (<a>) whose href points to a different domain than example.com; select the first such external link. 4) Record the exact href string (do not modify) and extract its domain (host component). 5) Return the source page URL as the citation.
details.verification_steps: Confirm the H1 extraction by checking the page's HTML; confirm the external link href is present verbatim in the page; confirm the external link domain is the host portion of that exact href.
details.rationale: This visit node directly collects the three requested pieces of evidence from example.com. Visiting the exact URL ensures we capture the authoritative page content and an exact URL citation for that page.
details.notes: Vector DB will be queried automatically for related stored links. Follow the Visit Action Requirements: copy exact URLs from the page; do not guess or construct URLs.

From today's featured article
Fort Southerland
is a
redoubt
built during the
American Civil War
to protect
Camden, Arkansas
.
Confederate
forces built it along with four other redoubts in early 1864 after a
Union
victory in the
Little Rock campaign
the previous year. Fort Southerland is about the size of a
city block
and is roughly oval. It could hold three cannons. When Union forces captured Camden in April 1864 during the
Camden Expedition
, they improved the defenses of the five redoubts, which were not sufficient for proper defense of the city. After the Confederates retook Camden later that month, they continued to improve the city's defenses. The fort lies within Fort Southerland Park, a municipal park dedicated in 1974. It was listed on the
National Register of Historic Places
in 1994, and is part of the
Camden Expedition Sites National Historic Landmark
. Along with
Fort Lookout
, it is one of only two of the redoubts around Camden still in existence.
(

Hurricane Ismael
The
1995 Pacific hurricane season
consisted of the events that occurred in the annual cycle of
tropical cyclone
formation over the
Pacific Ocean
north of the
equator
and east of the
International Date Line
. Activity during the
season
was below average; only eleven
tropical depressions
developed, of which ten strengthened into
named
tropical storms. Seven became
hurricanes
, of which three further intensified into major hurricanes. The first system, Tropical Depression
One-E
, formed on May 21; the final,
Hurricane Juliette
, dissipated on September 26. The vast majority of damage and casualties during the season resulted from
Hurricane Ismael
(pictured)
in mid-September. High seas damaged or destroyed fifty-two fishing boats, killing fifty-seven fishermen. On land, strong winds and severe flooding caused heavy damage and killed fifty-nine people in the Mexican state of
Sinaloa
. (

Hurricane Ismael
The
1995 Pacific hurricane season
consisted of the events that occurred in the annual cycle of
tropical cyclone
formation over the
Pacific Ocean
north of the
equator
and east of the
International Date Line
. Activity during the
season
was below average; only eleven
tropical depressions
developed, of which ten strengthened into
named
tropical storms. Seven became
hurricanes
, of which three further intensified into major hurricanes. The first system, Tropical Depression
One-E
, formed on May 21; the final,
Hurricane Juliette
, dissipated on September 26. The vast majority of damage and casualties during the season resulted from
Hurricane Ismael
(pictured)
in mid-September. High seas damaged or destroyed fifty-two fishing boats, killing fifty-seven fishermen. On land, strong winds and severe flooding caused heavy damage and killed fifty-nine people in the Mexican state of
Sinaloa
. (

From today's featured article
Fort Southerland
is a
redoubt
built during the
American Civil War
to protect
Camden, Arkansas
.
Confederate
forces built it along with four other redoubts in early 1864 after a
Union
victory in the
Little Rock campaign
the previous year. Fort Southerland is about the size of a
city block
and is roughly oval. It could hold three cannons. When Union forces captured Camden in April 1864 during the
Camden Expedition
, they improved the defenses of the five redoubts, which were not sufficient for proper defense of the city. After the Confederates retook Camden later that month, they continued to improve the city's defenses. The fort lies within Fort Southerland Park, a municipal park dedicated in 1974. It was listed on the
National Register of Historic Places
in 1994, and is part of the
Camden Expedition Sites National Historic Landmark
. Along with
Fort Lookout
, it is one of only two of the redoubts around Camden still in existence.
(

Hurricane Ismael
The
1995 Pacific hurricane season
consisted of the events that occurred in the annual cycle of
tropical cyclone
formation over the
Pacific Ocean
north of the
equator
and east of the
International Date Line
. Activity during the
season
was below average; only eleven
tropical depressions
developed, of which ten strengthened into
named
tropical storms. Seven became
hurricanes
, of which three further intensified into major hurricanes. The first system, Tropical Depression
One-E
, formed on May 21; the final,
Hurricane Juliette
, dissipated on September 26. The vast majority of damage and casualties during the season resulted from
Hurricane Ismael
(pictured)
in mid-September. High seas damaged or destroyed fifty-two fishing boats, killing fifty-seven fishermen. On land, strong winds and severe flooding caused heavy damage and killed fifty-nine people in the Mexican state of
Sinaloa
. (

e1cc5705-248d-4b62-a80d-e5358d048af4.title: Verify one adjacency pair by confirming that Hop 3 page contains an exact link to Hop 4 page (Adjacency verification)

details.optional_url: <exact hop3_page_url from node 'Select one link from Hop 2 page and visit that linked Wikipedia article (Hop 2 → 3)'>
details.intent: Prove adjacency for one pair near the end of the chain by showing the exact Hop 3 page contains an inline link whose exact URL equals the Hop 4 page URL.
target_facts: evidence_snippet (the exact '_links_inline' entry or a short extracted HTML/text snippet showing the exact URL)
details.extraction_plan: Visit the Hop 3 page (use the exact URL produced by the Hop 3 visit). Parse the page's inline links and search for an exact match with the Hop 4 page URL (as produced by the Hop 4 visit). Output a boolean adjacency_confirmed true/false and include the exact excerpt from the Hop 3 visit showing the link (for example the '_links_inline' entry or the exact line containing the link token).
details.justification: The mandate requests one verified adjacency pair near the end. Programmatic verification requires revisiting (or reusing) Hop 3's content and matching the exact Hop 4 URL string in its inline links. This node provides verifiable evidence (exact URL token) rather than a narrative claim.
details.goal: Verify one adjacency pair by confirming that Hop 3 page contains an exact link to Hop 4 page (Adjacency verification)
details.original_goal: Verify one adjacency pair by confirming that Hop 3 page contains an exact link to Hop 4 page (Adjacency verification)
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
